title: Feature request: Please provide a decent French model
author: stubhead
body:
### Use Cases

Current French speech-to-text models are deplorable.  They are inept to the point of mistaking the most rudimentary input ; to the point of rendering them even less useful than simply typing the input.  This includes the largest ["multilingual 244"] model.

**EXAMPLES :**
- confusing "faisant" with "faisons"
- confusing "frissons" with "fraisçons" [which is not even a real word !  ;p]
- confusing "haricots verts" with "arrêt couverts", "arikou verre", or again "arricot verre" [none of which makes sense and many of which are, also, not even real words in the French language...]

### Proposal

Please find -- or take the time to create -- a reasonable model that actually works well.  Seriously, the multilingual 244 is unacceptable for both German and French, but especially French.

### References

_No response_

title: Chinese voice input outputs Traditional Chinese; please add Simplified Chinese / zh-Hans option
author: msjsc001
body:
### Use Cases

I am using FUTO Voice Input for Chinese voice dictation on Android.

Issue:
When I select Chinese and use offline voice input, the recognized text is output in Traditional Chinese. I could not find an option to force Simplified Chinese output.

Expected behavior:
Please add a setting for Chinese output script, for example:
- Chinese Simplified / zh-Hans / zh-CN
- Chinese Traditional / zh-Hant / zh-TW / zh-HK

Actual behavior:
Chinese voice input currently outputs Traditional Chinese for me, which makes it difficult to use in Mainland Chinese / Simplified Chinese workflows.

Why this matters:
FUTO Voice Input is very easy to configure and works well offline, but without Simplified Chinese output it is hard to use as a daily Chinese voice input tool.

Device / environment:
- Android version:
- FUTO Voice Input version:
- Model used: Multilingual-74 / Multilingual-244 / other
- System language: Simplified Chinese, China
- Keyboard used: Yuyan IME / other

Suggestion:
If the ASR model cannot directly control script output, a post-processing option using zh-Hans normalization or OpenCC-style Traditional-to-Simplified conversion before inserting text would solve the problem.

### Proposal

_No response_

### References

_No response_

title: Feature request: Would like Futo to work alongside Microsoft Teams.
author: corderoau
body:
### Use Cases

In Microsoft Teams, I use the chat feature a lot. It's one of the main ways of communicating. However, for some strange reason, it does not seem to accept input from photo. Is there any way around this? 

### Proposal

_No response_

title: Feature request: ability to operate Facebook Messenger with Futo.
author: corderoau
body:
### Use Cases

For some strange reason when operating Futo and Facebook Messenger together, I find the response text that I speak gets delivered out of sequence. Some words get delayed until the very end. Not sure why that is. Maybe it's delivering real time. Maybe it needs to buffer first. In any case, I think it's a fantastic product.

### Proposal

Is it possible that once Futo is triggered it takes control of the app completely until such time as it has delivered the entire message and then deliver it as if it were typed in. incrementally sending it to Facebook messenger probably causes the issue.

### References

_No response_

title: Bug: downloads not working
author: cmroanirgo
body:
### Reproduction steps

When I choose a model other than the default, it never actually downloads. I have tried with both English-74 and 244.

1. Choose a non default model & click continue after the filename sitars onscreen. 
2. Nothing appears to happen and you leave it to (perhaps) download in the background. You only see an empty progress bar.
3. Minutes or hours later you open voice input either directly or via heliboard and you are prompted to download again.
4. I use NetGuard and it's logs show two requests to dl.voiceinput.futo.org and no retries on port 5066 &5067. I have tried also with NetGuard disabled.

### Actual result

I always get prompted to download the model. If I change back to the default model the app works, but using the default model 

### Expected result

I expect some UI feedback and retries to download.

### Voice Input Version

1 3.7-2

### Which android version are you using?

Android 15

### Which device are you using?

moto g54

### Which operating system are you using?

stock android

### References

_No response_


title: Feature request: undo last input
author: Ronkn
body:
### Use Cases

Typically voice to text is used when you're unable to use your hands for input. Often with any voice to text including this one, it misunderstands you and the wrong words are input. This results in manually deleting or editing the input text with your hands, defeating the purpose of this app. 

### Proposal

It would be useful to have a quick undo feature or confirmation showing you what was heard, before it's input. Maybe give you the chance to say no, and then automatically prompts to re-input your voice to text. 

### References

No. I didn't find anything with the keyword "undo"

title: Bug: Inputs sometimes fail if used more than once in the same app
author: Hellreaver
body:
### Reproduction steps

1. Attempt to use FUTO Voice Input to fill a field in an app.
2. Succeed once.
3. Attempt again to use FUTO Voice to fill a field in the same app, without switching
4. Failure. Text is generated but not pasted. 

### Actual result

Voice input fails to fill the field if used more than once. This seems to be related to the text box moving or being displaced in some way. I'm seeing this behavior on YouTube and in Facebook Messenger, but not on GitHub.

I was just forcibly updated to CP1A.260305.018. 

### Expected result

It was supposed to correctly fill the field with text that it had generated.

### Voice Input Version

1.3.7-1

Android 16

Google Pixel 8 Pro

### Which operating system are you using?

Stock pixel OS

title: Bug: Payment process does not start, returns error related to network permission
author: kazuhiro-yasei
body:
### Reproduction steps

1. Open FUTO Voice Input
2. In the Settings menu, with the 'Unpaid FUTO Voice Input' message, tap on 'Pay Now'
3. On its following page, again tap 'Pay'
4. Error message pops up.

### Actual result

The following error message immediately shows up: "Failed to get required payment data (did you grant the app network permission?)", with an URL beneath it: https://free.freeipapi.com/api/json

[Here is a video](https://youtube.com/shorts/ai5AOeTFRtE) I've uploaded to YouTube (because GitHub wasn't allowing me to upload it directly here - I take the 55,1 MB file size is the issue?) to demonstrate the process and error, as well as the lack of a toggleable network permission for the application (the only mention of a network-related permission is under the 'All permissions' subpage, in which none of the displayed ones is toggleable).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f7336a22-a6ca-4079-b064-a431364940e6)

### Expected result

A window with a payment flow should pop up, allowing the user to input credit card info.

### Voice Input Version

1.3.6

### Which android version are you using?

Android 15

### Which device are you using?

Infinix Note 40 5G

### Which operating system are you using?

XOS v15.1.2

_No response_

title: Feature request: Home Button Long-Press Launcher
author: mattvisa
body:
### Use Cases

There is no viable version of a home button launcher that is not proprietary big tech that I am aware of after searching the Google App Store or F-Droid.



### Proposal

This can be so helpful to transition voice-input into a cell phone "home button long-press launcher" that will simply

1. launch voice-input to open up the voice recording
2. record voice
3. pipe the voice command into your default mobile browser and hit enter to search the default search engine of said browser. 

That's it. 

Just do that and this app becomes so useful and a sort of version of Alexa or Siri or whatever. 

### References

_No response_

title: Feature request: Add Australian (en‑AU) or UK (en‑GB) English Speech‑to‑Text Model
author: Unglazed-pixel
body:
### Use Cases

FUTO Voice Input currently outputs American spelling and is tuned to American pronunciation. For users with  device language set to English (Australia) or English (GB), the transcription consistently produces American spellings (e.g., color, organize, traveling, license) instead of Commonwealth spelling.

This creates usability issues in everyday writing, work communication and any context where correct Australian/British spelling is required. It also reduces recognition accuracy for these accents, since the model is not trained on Australian or British phonetics.

The core problem is that there is no available English model that matches Commonwealth spelling or Australian/British pronunciation, even when the system language is set accordingly.

### Proposal

Add support for Australian English (en‑AU) and/or UK English (en‑GB) speech‑to‑text models. Either variant would resolve the spelling issue, as Australian and British spelling conventions are effectively identical for most words. Both would also improve recognition accuracy for users with Australian, British, or mixed accents.
How this could look in the application
- In FUTO Voice Input → Languages / Models, provide downloadable models for:
- English (Australia)
- English (United Kingdom)
- When the device language is set to en‑AU or en‑GB, FUTO Voice Input should automatically select the matching model.
- Users could still manually choose a model if they prefer a different variant.

Notes on precedent
Many widely used speech engines (including Google, Apple, and Microsoft) offer separate en‑AU and en‑GB models. These variants provide correct Commonwealth spelling and improved recognition for local accents. Adding similar options would bring FUTO Voice Input in line with common behaviour across other speech‑to‑text systems.

I’m happy to provide sample audio or help test early builds if that would be useful.

### References

_No response_

title: Feature request: Ability to manually stop recording
author: twiecki
body:
### Use Cases

When dictating I like to speak long monologues where I take long thinking pauses which causes the silence detector to stop recording and stop my message prematurely.

### Proposal

Maybe there was a setting or a way to just keep the button pressed as long as I'm talking, or any way I can only have the recording stop when I actually press a button or perform an action, not automatically.

### References

_No response_

title: Feature request: Consider implementation of Parakeet v3 model
author: Choupinounours
body:
### Use Cases

Whisper model may be inaccurate.

### Proposal

Hello,

On my computer, i use [Handy](https://handy.computer) as a Speech-to-Text converter, with the model Parakeet v3.

The latter feels more accurate for me (for french language, in my case) comparing to whisper models, and is also lightweight.

Could it be feasible (and accurate) to propose this model in Voice Input ?

Thank you (and thank you for all the work you did for voice input and FUTO keyboard !)

### References

_No response_

title: Feature request: File based personal dictionary input
author: rambalachandran
body:
### Use Cases

The current approach of using personal dictionary with FUTO voice, is that you need to manually input into a text box, this is both time consuming and very restrictive, especially if you have a lot of custom words like proper nouns that are typically not going to be present in the training. It becomes very useful if you have a file based input. This file can be maintained outside and can be appended and just uploaded. voice, is that you need to manually input into a text box, this is both time consuming and very restrictive, especially if you want to use it, where you have a lot of custom words that are typically not going to be present in the training, it becomes very useful if you have a file based input.

### Proposal

Implement an approach where it is a simple file uploaded as a text file or any other format that makes it easier for the voice to process them and add them when doing ASR.

### References

_No response_

title: Bug: Substandard sentence/paragraph structure, and "Thank you" being added to end of text
author: inspiredearth
body:
### Reproduction steps

1. Dictate a long message in English
2. View the output

### Actual result

1. Very long sentences with run-on sentence structure. Excessive use of commas where there should be full-stops/periods.
2. There is zero paragraph structure.
3. The words "Thank you" often get added to the end of the transcription.

### Expected result

1. Properly structured sentences, that show an underlying recognition of what has been said so that the content is formatted correctly. They should be grammatically correct.
2. No addition of "Thank you" at the end of my dictations.
3. Ideally, paragraph structure for longer dictations, where grammatically appropriate.

### Voice Input Version

1.3.6

### Which android version are you using?

Android 16

### Which device are you using?

Samsung Galaxy A36 5G

### Which operating system are you using?

Samsung One UI 8

### References

I can't say if this is a bug per se, or a feature that needs adding.

Currently, I find use of FUTO results in very poor sentence structure, and no paragraph structure. 

I use Whisper a lot (and have done so for a couple of years). FUTO's output is perhaps the poorest I've encountered. It's as though the underlying AI is putting very little "effort" into understanding what's been said in order to apply appropriate structure. I typically end up with _very_ long sentences, that use a lot of commas to keep running one part onto the last. Situations where clearly a full-stop/period should be used. I then have to spend a considerable amount of time correcting the output.

**I suspect the underlying system prompt needs refining or improving.**

**As a feature request**, I suggest the system prompt be user editable. That way we can set the prompt to something that works for us.

I see there has been [a feature request](https://github.com/futo-org/voice-input/issues/20) for FUTO to use no punctuation and instead act on formatting instructions. Being able to edit the prompt would allow users who want this behaviour to simply modify the prompt accordingly.

The other odd thing is that very often the output is adding "Thank you" to the end of the transcription. I have no idea why, and can only assume it's a quirk arising from a poorly structured system prompt. It's not something I've encountered on the many other implementations of Whisper I use on a daily basis.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

title: Bug: Too many time formats in German
author: jochenkonietzko-cell

### Reproduction steps

1. Use FUTO Voice
2. Say, for example, "fünf Uhr neunundfünfzig"
3. Marvel at the variety

Received...

5 Uhr 59
fünf Uhr 59
5 Uhr neunundfünfzig
5 Uhr neun und fünfzig
fünf Uhr neun und fünftzig
5,59 Uhr
5.59 Uhr
5 Uhr 59 Uhr 
5-59

### Expected result

Consistency! Any format with digits would be okay, but only ONE, please:
5:59  or  5:59 Uhr   or 5.59  or  5.59 Uhr

1.3.7-1

Android 10

Samsung Galaxy S9

Android, rooted
